book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
The Last Farewell in the Presence of the One Hundred and Fifty Bishops.

ccel.title: NPNF2-07. Cyril of Jerusalem, Gregory Nazianzen

source.title: NPNF2-07. Cyril of Jerusalem, Gregory Nazianzen

title: The Last Farewell in the Presence of the One Hundred and Fifty Bishops.

source.title: NPNF2-07. Cyril of Jerusalem, Gregory Nazianzen

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: ThisOration was delivered during the Second Œcumenical Council, held at Constantinople a.d.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Historical as well as personal motives render the occasion of the deepest interest.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The audience consisted of the one hundred and fifty Bishops of the Eastern Church who took part in the Council, and of the speaker’s own flock, the orthodox Christians of Constantinople.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: He had by his own exertions gathered that flock together, after it had been ravaged by heretical teachers.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: He had won the admiration and affection of its members, by his courageous championship of the Faith, his lucid teaching, and his fatherly care for their spiritual needs.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: He had been, against his will, enthroned with acclamation in the highest ecclesiastical position in the Eastern Church, and called to preside over the Synod of its assembled Bishops.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Finding himself unable to guide the deliberations of the Council in regard to a question of the highest importance, and perceiving that he himself and his position were made by some of the Bishops a fresh cause of dissension, he felt bound to resign his high office, and endeavour by this personal sacrifice to restore peace to the Church.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: His language is worthy of the occasion.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Obliged to deal with the topics which had caused dissension, he handles them with gentle and discriminating tact; he speaks with great self-restraint in his own defence; he sets forth with tenderest feeling the common experiences of himself and his flock; he gives with dignity and clearness his last public exposition of the Faith; and finally, in language of exquisite beauty, spoken with the quivering tones of an aged man, he bids a tender farewell to his flock, his cathedral, and his throne, with all their affecting associations.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: It was an occasion whose pathos is unsurpassed in history.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Orator and audience were alike deeply moved, and the emotion has been renewed in all those who have read his words, and realised the scene of their delivery.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What think ye of our affairs, dear shepherds and fellow-shepherds: whose feet are beautiful, for you bring glad tidings of peace and of the good things with which ye have come; beautiful again in our eyes, to whom ye have come in season, not to convert a wandering sheep, but to converse with a pilgrim shepherd?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What think ye of this our pilgrimage?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And of its fruit, or rather of that of the Spirit within us, by Whom we are ever moved, and specially have now been moved, desiring to have, and perhaps having, nothing of our own?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Do you of yourselves understand and perceive—and are you kindly critics of our actions?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Or must we, like those from whom a reckoning is demanded as to their military command, or civil government, or administration of the exchequer, publicly and in person submit to you the accounts of our administration?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Not indeed that we are ashamed of being judged, for we are ourselves judges in turn, and both with the same charity.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But the law is an ancient one: for even Paul communicated to the Apostles his Gospel: not for the sake of ostentation, for the Spirit is far removed from all ostentation, but in order to establish his success and correct his failure, if indeed there were any such in his words or actions, as he declares when writing of himself.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Since even the Spirits of the Prophets are subject to the prophets, according to the order of the Spirit who regulates and divides all things well.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And do not wonder that, while he rendered his account privately and to some, I do so publicly, and to all.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For my need is greater than his, of being aided by the freedom of my censors, if I am proved to have failed in my duty, lest I should run, or have run, in vain.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And the only possible mode of self-defence is speech in the presence of men who know the facts.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What then is my defence?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If it be false, you must convict me, but if true, you on behalf of whom and in whose presence I speak, must bear witness to it.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For you are my defence, my witnesses, and my crown of rejoicing, if I also may venture to boast myself a little in the Apostle’s language.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: This flock was, when it was small and poor, as far as appearances went, nay, not even a flock, but a slight trace and relic of a flock, without order, or shepherd, or bounds, with neither right to pasturage, nor the defence of a fold, wandering upon the mountains and in caves and dens of the earth, scattered and dispersed hither and thither as each one could find shelter or pasture, or could gratefully secure its own safety; like that flock which was harassed by lions, dispersed by tempest, or scattered in darkness, the lamentation of prophets who compared it to the misfortunes of Israel, given up to the Gentiles; over which we also lamented, so long as our lot was worthy of lamentation.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For in very deed we also were thrust out and cast off, and scattered upon every mountain and hill, from the need of a shepherd: and a dreadful storm fell upon the Church, and fearful beasts assailed her, who do not even now, after the calm, spare us, but without being ashamed of themselves, wield a greater power than the time should allow; while a gloomy darkness, far more oppressive than the ninth plague of Egypt, the darkness which might be felt, enveloped and concealed everything, so that we could scarcely even see one another.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: To speak in a more feeling strain, trusting in Him Who then forsook me, as in a Father, “Abraham has been ignorant of us, Israel has acknowledged us not, but Thou art our Father, and unto Thee do we look; beside Thee we know none else, we make mention of Thy name.”

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Therefore, says Jeremiah, I will plead with Thee, I will reason the cause with Thee.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: We are become as at the beginning, when Thou barest not rule over us, and Thou hast forgotten Thy holy covenant, and shut up Thy mercies from us.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Therefore we, the worshippers of the Trinity, the perfect suppliants of the perfect Deity, became a reproach to Thy Beloved, neither daring to bring down to our own level any of the things above us, nor in such wise to rise up against the godless tongues which fought against God, as to make His Majesty a fellow servant with ourselves; but, as is plain, we were delivered up on account of our other sins, and because our conduct had been unworthy of Thy commandments, and we had walked after our own evil mind.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For what other reason can there be for our being delivered up to the most unrighteous and wicked men of all the dwellers upon the earth?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: First Nebuchadnezzar afflicted us, possessed during the Christian era with an anti-Christian rage, hating Christ just because he had through Him gained salvation, and having bartered the sacred books for sacrifices to those who are no gods.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: He devoured me, he tore me in pieces, a slight darkness enveloped me, if I may even in my lamentation keep to the language of Scripture.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If the Lord had not helped me, and righteously delivered him to the hands of the lawless, by casting him off (such are the judgments of God) to the Persians, by whom his blood was righteously shed for his unholy sheddings of blood, since in this case alone justice could not afford even to be longsuffering, my soul had shortly dwelt in the grave.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The second no more kindly, if he were not even more grievous still, for while he bore the name of Christ, he was a false Christ, and at once a burden and a reproach to the Christians, for, while to obey him was ungodly, to suffer at his hands was inglorious, since they did not even seem to be wronged, nor to gain by their sufferings the glorious title of martyr, inasmuch as the truth was in this case perverted, for while they suffered as Christians, they were supposed to be punished as heretics.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Alas! how rich we were in misfortunes, for the fire consumed the beauties of the world.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: That which the palmerworm left did the locust eat, and that which the locust left did the caterpillar eat: then came the cankerworm, then, what next I know not, one evil springing up after another.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But for what purpose should I give a tragic description of the evils of the time, and of the penalty exacted from us, or, if I must rather call it so, the testing and refining we endured?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: At any rate, we went through fire and water, and have attained a place of refreshment by the good pleasure of God our Saviour.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: To return to my original startingpoint.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: This was my field, when it was small and poor, unworthy not only of God, Who has been, and is cultivating the whole world with the fair seeds and doctrines of piety, but, apparently, even of any poor and needy man of slender means.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Nay it did not deserve to be called a field, requiring neither barn nor threshing-floor, and not even worthy of the sickle; with neither heap nor sheaves, or small and untimely sheaves, like those on the housetop, which do not fill the hand of the reaper, nor call forth a blessing from them which go by.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Such was my field, such my harvest; great and well-eared and fat in the eyes of Him Who beholdeth hidden things, and becoming such a husbandman, its abundance springing from the valleys of souls well tilled with the Word: unrecognized however in public, and not collected together, but gathered in fragments, as an ear gleaned in the stubble, as gleaning-grapes in the vintage, where there is no cluster left.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I think I may add, only too appropriately, I found Israel like a figtree in the wilderness, and like one or two ripe grapes in an unripe cluster, preserved as a blessing from the Lord, and a consecrated firstfruit, though small as yet and scanty, and not filling the mouth of the eater: and as an ensign on a hill, and as a beacon on a mountain, or any other solitary thing visible only to few.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Such was its former poverty and dejection.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But since God, Who maketh poor and maketh rich, Who killeth and maketh alive; Who maketh and transformeth all things; Who turneth night into day, winter into spring, storm into calm, drought into abundance of rain; and often for the sake of the prayers of one righteous man sorely persecuted; Who lifteth up the meek on high, and bringeth the ungodly down to the ground; since God said to Himself, I have surely seen the affliction of Israel; and they shall no longer be further vexed with clay and brick-making; and when He spake He visited, and in His visitation He saved, and led forth His people with a mighty hand and outstretched arm, by the hand of Moses and Aaron, His chosen—what is the result, and what wonders have been wrought?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Those which books and monuments contain.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For besides all the wonders by the way, and that mighty roar, to speak most concisely, Joseph came into Egypt alone, and soon after six hundred thousand depart from Egypt.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What more marvellous than this?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What greater proof of the generosity of God, when from men without means He wills to supply the means for public affairs?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And the land of promise is distributed through one who was hated, and he who was sold dispossesses nations, and is himself made a great nation, and that small offshoot becomes a luxuriant vine, so great that it reaches to the river, and is stretched out to the sea, and spreads from border to border, and hides the mountains with the height of its glory and is exalted above the cedars, even the cedars of God, whatever we are to take these mountains and cedars to be.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Such then was once this flock, and such it is now, so healthy and well grown, and if it be not yet in perfection, it is advancing towards it by constant increase, and I prophesy that it will advance.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: This is foretold me by the Holy Spirit, if I have any prophetic instinct and insight into the future.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And from what has preceded I am able to be confident, and recognize this by reasoning, being the nursling of reason.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For it was much more improbable that, from that condition, it should reach its present development, than that, as it now is, it should attain to the height of renown.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For ever since it began to be gathered together, by Him Who quickeneth the dead, bone to its bone, joint to joint, and the Spirit of life and regeneration was given to it in their dryness, its entire resurrection has been, I know well, sure to be fulfilled: so that the rebellious should not exalt themselves, and that those who grasp at a shadow, or at a dream when one awaketh, or at the dispersing breezes, or at the traces of a ship in the water, should not think that they have anything.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Howl, firtree, for the cedar is fallen!

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Let them be instructed by the misfortunes of others, and learn that the poor shall not alway be forgotten, and that the Deity will not refrain, as Habakkuk says, from striking through the heads of the mighty ones in His fury—the Deity, Who has been struck through and impiously divided into Ruler and Ruled, in order to insult the Deity in the highest degree by degrading It, and oppress a creature by equality with Deity.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I seem indeed to hear that voice, from Him Who gathers together those who are broken, and welcomes the oppressed: Enlarge thy cords, break forth on the right hand and on the left, drive in thy stakes, spare not thy curtains.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I have given thee up, and I will help thee.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: In a little wrath I smote thee, but with everlasting mercy I will glorify thee.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The measure of His kindness exceeds the measure of His discipline.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The former things were owing to our wickedness, the present things to the adorable Trinity: the former for our cleansing, the present for My glory, Who will glorify them that glorify Me, and I will move to jealousy them that move Me to jealousy.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Behold this is sealed up with Me, and this is the indissoluble law of recompense.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But thou didst surround thyself with walls and tablets and richly set stones, and long porticos and galleries, and didst shine and sparkle with gold, which thou didst, in part pour forth like water, in part treasure up like sand; not knowing that better is faith, with no other roof but the sky to cover it, than impiety rolling in wealth, and that three gathered together in the Name of the Lord count for more with God than tens of thousands of those who deny the Godhead.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Would you prefer the whole of the Canaanites to Abraham alone? or the men of Sodom to Lot? or the Midianites to Moses, when each of these was a pilgrim and a stranger?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: How do the three hundred men with Gideon, who bravely lapped, compare with the thousands who were put to flight?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Or the servants of Abraham, who scarcely exceeded them in number, with the many kings and the army of tens of thousands whom, few as they were, they overtook and defeated?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Or how do you understand the passage that though the number of the children of Israel be as the sand of the sea, a remnant shall be saved?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And again, I have left me seven thousand men, who have not bowed the knee to Baal?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: This is not the case; it is not?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: God has not taken pleasure in numbers.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Thou countest tens of thousands, God counts those who are in a state of salvation; thou countest the dust which is without number, I the vessels of election.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For nothing is so magnificent in God’s sight as pure doctrine, and a soul perfect in all the dogmas of the truth.—For there is nothing worthy of Him Who made all things, of Him by Whom are all things, and for Whom are all things, so that it can be given or offered to God: not merely the handiwork or means of any individual, but even if we wished to honour Him, by uniting together all the property and handiwork of all mankind.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Do not I fill heaven and earth? saith the Lord! and what house will ye build Me? or what is the place of My rest?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But, since man must needs fall short of what is worthy, I ask of you, as approaching it most nearly, piety, the wealth which is common to all and equal in My eyes, wherein the poorest may, if he be nobleminded, surpass the most illustrious.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For this kind of glory depends upon purpose, not upon affluence.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: These things be well assured, I will accept at your hands.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: To tread My courts ye shall not proceed, but the feet of the meek shall tread them, who have duly and sincerely acknowledged Me, and My only-begotten Word, and the Holy Spirit.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: How long will ye inherit My holy Mountain?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: How long shall My ark be among the heathen?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Now for a little longer ye indulge yourselves in that which belongs to others, and gratify your desires.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For as ye have devised to reject Me, so will I also reject you, saith the Lord Almighty.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: This I seemed to hear Him say, and to see Him do, and besides, to hear Him shouting to His people, which once were few and scattered and miserable, and have now become many, and compact enough and enviable, Go through My gates and be ye enlarged.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Must you always be in trouble and dwell in tents, while those who vex you rejoice exceedingly?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And to the presiding Angels, for I believe, as John teaches me in his Revelation, that each Church has its guardian, Prepare ye the way of My people, and cast away the stones from the way, that there may be no stumblingblock or hindrance for the people in the divine road and entrance, now, to the temples made with hands, but soon after, to Jerusalem above, and the Holy of holies there, which will, I know, be the end of suffering and struggle to those who here bravely travel on the way.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Among whom are ye also called to be Saints, a people of possession, a royal priesthood, the most excellent portion of the Lord, a whole river from a drop, a heavenly lamp from a spark, a tree from a grain of mustard seed, on which the birds come and lodge.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: These we present to you, dear shepherds, these we offer to you, with these we welcome our friends, and guests, and fellow pilgrims.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: We have nothing fairer or more splendid to offer to you, for we have selected the greatest of all our possessions, that you may see that, strangers as we are, we are not in want, but though poor are making many rich.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If these things are small and unworthy of notice, I would fain learn what is greater and of more account.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For, if it be no great thing to have established and strengthened with wholesome doctrines a city which is the eye of the universe, in its exceeding strength by sea and land, which is, as it were, the link between the Eastern and Western shores, in which the extremities of the world from every side meet together, and from which, as the common mart of the faith, they take their rise, a city borne hither and thither on the eddying currents of so many tongues, it will be long ere anything be considered great or worthy of esteem.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But if it be indeed a subject for praise, allow to us some glory on this account, since we have contributed in some portion to these results which ye see.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Lift up thine eyes round about, and see, thou critic of my words!

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: See the crown which has been platted in return for the hirelings of Ephraim and the crown of insolence; see the assembly of the presbyters, honoured for years and wisdom, the fair order of the deacons, who are not far from the same Spirit, the good conduct of the readers, the people’s eagerness for teaching, both of men and women, who are equally renowned for virtue: the men, whether philosophers or simple folk, being alike wise in divine things, whether rulers or ruled, being all in this respect duly under rule; whether soldiers or nobles, students or men of letters, being all soldiers of God, though in all other respects meek, ready to fight for the Spirit, all reverencing the assembly above, to which we obtain an entrance, not by the mere letter, but by the quickening Spirit, all in very deed being men of reason, and worshippers of Him Who is in truth the Word: the women, if married, being united by a Divine rather than by a carnal bond; if unwedded and free, being entirely dedicated to God; whether young or old, some honourably advancing towards old age, others eagerly striving to remain immortal, being renewed by the best of hopes.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: To those who platted this crown—that which I speak, I speak it not after the Lord, nevertheless I will say it—I also have given assistance.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Some of them are the result of my words, not of those which we have uttered at random, but of those which we have loved—nor again of those which are meretricious, though the language and manners of the harlot have been slanderously attributed to me, but of those which are most grave.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Some of them are the offspring and fruit of my Spirit, as the Spirit can beget those who rise superior to the body.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: To this I have no doubt that those who are kindly among you, nay all of you, will testify, since I have been the husbandman of all: and my sole reward is your confession.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For we neither have, nor have had, any other object.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For virtue, that it may remain virtue, is without reward, its eyes fixed alone on that which is good.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Would you have me say something still more venturesome?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Do you see the tongues of the enemy made gentle, and those who made war upon the Godhead against me tranquillised?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: This also is the result of our Spirit, of our husbandry.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For we are not undisciplined in our exercise of discipline, nor do we hurl insults, as many do, who assail not the argument but the speaker, and sometimes strive by their invective to hide the weakness of their reasoning; as the cuttlefish are said to cast forth ink before them, in order to escape from their pursuers, or themselves to hunt others when unperceived.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But we show that our warfare is in behalf of Christ by fighting as Christ, the peaceable and meek, Who has borne our infirmities, fought.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Though peaceable, we do not injure the word of truth, by yielding a jot, to gain a reputation for reasonableness; for we do not pursue that which is good by means of ill: and we are peaceable by the legitimate character of our warfare, confined as it is to our own limits, and the rules of the Spirit.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Upon these points, this is my decision, and I lay down the law for all stewards of souls and dispensers of the Word: neither to exasperate others by their harshness, nor to render them arrogant by submissiveness: but to be of good words in treating of the Word, and in neither direction to overstep the mean.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But you are perhaps longing for me to give an exposition of the faith, in so far as I am able.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For I shall myself be sanctified by the effort of memory, and the people also will be benefited, by its special delight in such discussions, and you will fully acknowledge it—unless we are the objects of groundless envy, as the rivals, in the manifestation of the truth, of those whom we do not excel.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For as, of deep waters, some in the depths are utterly hidden, some foam against any obstruction, and hesitate a while before breaking (as they promise to our ears), some do actually break; so also, of those who are professors of the Divine philosophy—setting aside the utterly misguided—some keep their piety entirely secret and hidden within themselves, some are not far from the birth pangs, avoiding impiety, yet not speaking out their piety, either from cautious reserve in their teaching, or under pressure of fear, being themselves sound, as they say, in mind, but not making sound their people, as if they had been entrusted with the government of their own souls, but not of those of others; while there are some who make public their treasure, unable to restrain themselves from giving birth to their piety, and not considering that to be salvation which saves themselves alone, without bestowing upon others the overflow of their blessings.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Among these would I range myself, and all who by my side have nobly dared to confess the truth.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: One concise proclamation of our teaching, an inscription intelligible to all, is this people, which so sincerely worships the Trinity, that it would sooner sever anyone from this life, than sever one of the three from the Godhead: of one mind, of equal zeal, and united to one another, to us and to the Trinity by unity of doctrine.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Briefly to run over its details: That which is without beginning, and is the beginning, and is with the beginning, is one God.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For the nature of that which is without beginning does not consist in being without beginning or being unbegotten, for the nature of anything lies, not in what it is not but in what it is.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: It is the assertion of what is, not the denial of what is not.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And the Beginning is not, because it is a beginning, separated from that which has no beginning.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For its beginning is not its nature, any more than the being without beginning is the nature of the other.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For these are the accompaniments of the nature, not the nature itself.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: That again which is with that which has no beginning, and with the beginning, is not anything else than what they are.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Now, the name of that which has no beginning is the Father, and of the Beginning the Son, and of that which is with the Beginning, the Holy Ghost, and the three have one Nature—God.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And the union is the Father from Whom and to Whom the order of Persons runs its course, not so as to be confounded, but so as to be possessed, without distinction of time, of will, or of power.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For these things in our case produce a plurality of individuals, since each of them is separate both from every other quality, and from every other individual possession of the same quality.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But to Those who have a simple nature, and whose essence is the same, the term One belongs in its highest sense.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Let us then bid farewell to all contentious shiftings and balancings of the truth on either side, neither, like the Sabellians, assailing the Trinity in the interest of the Unity, and so destroying the distinction by a wicked confusion; nor, like the Arians, assailing the Unity in the interest of the Trinity, and by an impious distinction overthrowing the Oneness.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For our object is not to exchange one evil for another, but to ensure our attainment of that which is good.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: These are the playthings of the Wicked One, who is ever swaying our fortunes towards the evil.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But we, walking along the royal road which lies between the two extremes, which is the seat of the virtues, as the authorities say, believe in the Father, the Son and the Holy Ghost, of one Substance and glory; in Whom also baptism has its perfection, both nominally and really (thou knowest who hast been initiated!); being a denial of atheism and a confession of Godhead; and thus we are regenerated, acknowledging the Unity in the Essence and in the undivided worship, and the Trinity in the Hypostases or Persons (which term some prefer.)

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And let not those who are contentious on these points utter their scandalous taunts, as if our faith depended on terms and not on realities.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For what do you mean who assert the three Hypostases?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Do you imply three Essences by the term?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I am assured that you would loudly shout against those who do so.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For you teach that the Essence of the Three is One and the same.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What do you mean, who assert the Three Persons?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Do you imagine a single compound sort of being, with three faces, or of an entirely human form?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Perish the thought!

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: You too will loudly reply that he who thinks thus, will never see the face of God, whatever it may be.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What is the meaning of the Hypostases of the one party, of the Persons of the other, to ask this further question?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: That They are three, Who are distinguished not by natures, but by properties.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: How could men agree and harmonize better than you do, even if there be a difference between the syllables you use?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: You see what a reconciler I am, bringing you back from the letter to the sense, as we do with the Old and New Testaments.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But, to resume: let us speak of the Unbegotten, the Begotten, and the Proceeding, if anyone likes to create names: for we shall have no fear of bodily conceptions attaching to Those who are not embodied, as the calumniators of the Godhead think.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For the creature must be called God’s, and this is for us a great thing, but God never.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Otherwise I shall admit that God is a creature, if I become God, in the strict sense of the term.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For this is the truth.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If God, He is not a creature; for the creature ranks with us who are not Gods.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And if a creature, he is not God, for he had a beginning in time.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And there was a time when he who had a beginning was not.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And that of which non-existence was its prior condition, has not being in the strict sense of the term.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And how can that, which strictly has not being, be God?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Not one single one, then, of the Three is a creature, nor, what is worse, came into being for my sake; for in that case he would be not only a creature, but inferior in honour to us.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For, if I am for the glory of God, and he is for my sake, as the tongs for the waggon, the saw for the door, I am his superior in causality.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For in whatever degree God is superior to creatures, in the same degree is he, who came into being for my sake, inferior to me who exist for God’s sake.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Moreover, the Moabites and Ammonites must not even be allowed to enter into the Church of God, I mean those sophistical, mischievous arguments which enquire curiously into the generation and inexpressible procession of God, and rashly set themselves in array against the Godhead: as if it were necessary that those things which it is beyond the power of language to set forth, must either be accessible to them alone, or else have no existence because they have not comprehended them.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: We however, following the Divine Scriptures, and removing out of the way of the blind the stumbling blocks contained in them, will cling to salvation, daring any and every thing rather than arrogance against God.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: As for the evidences, we leave them to others, since they have been set forth by many, and by ourselves also with no little care.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And indeed, it would be a very shameful thing for me at this time to be gathering together proofs for what has all along been believed.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For it is not the best order of things, first to teach and then to learn, even in matters which are small and of no consequence, and much more in those which are Divine and of such great importance.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Nor, again, is it proper to the present occasion to explain and disentangle the difficulties of Scripture, a task requiring fuller and more careful consideration than our present purpose will allow.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Such then, to sum up, is our teaching.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I have entered into these details, with no intention of contending against the adversaries: for I have already often, even if it be imperfectly, fought out the question with them: but in order that I might exhibit to you the character of my teaching, that you might see whether I have not a share in the defence of your own, and do not take my stand on the same side, and opposed to the same enemies as yourselves.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: You have now, my friends, heard the defence of my presence here: if it be deserving of praise, thanks are due for it to God, and to you who called me; if it has fallen below your expectation, I give thanks even on this behalf.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For I am assured that it has not been altogether deserving of censure, and am confident that you also admit this.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Have we at all made a gain of this people?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Have we consulted at all our own interests, as I see is most often the case?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Have we caused any vexation to the Church?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: To others possibly, with whose idea that they had gained judgment against us by default, we have joined issue in our argument; but in no wise, as far as I am aware, to you.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I have taken no ox of yours, says the great Samuel, in his contention against Israel on the subject of the king, nor any propitiation for your souls, the Lord is witness among you, nor this, nor that, proceeding at greater length, that I may not count up every particular; but I have kept the priesthood pure and unalloyed.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And if I have loved power, or the height of a throne, or to tread Kings’ courts, may I never possess any distinction, or if I gain it, may I be hurled from it.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What then do I mean?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I am no proficient in virtue without reward, having not attained to so high a degree of virtue.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Give me the reward of my labours.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What reward?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Not that which some, prone to any suspicion would suppose, but that which it is safe for me to seek.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Give me a respite from my long labours; give honour to my foreign service; elect another in my place, the one who is being eagerly sought on your behalf, someone who is clean of hands, someone who is not unskilled in voice, someone who is able to gratify you on all points, and share with you the ecclesiastical cares; for this is especially the time for such.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But behold, I pray you, the condition of this body, so drained by time, by disease, by toil.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What need have you of a timid and unmanly old man, who is, so to speak, dying day by day, not only in body, but even in powers of mind, who finds it difficult to enter into these details before you?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Disobey not the voice of your teacher: for indeed you have never yet disobeyed it.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I am weary of being charged with my gentleness.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I am weary of being assailed in words and in envy by enemies, and by our own.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Some aim at my breast, and are less successful in their effort, for an open enemy can be guarded against.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Others lie in wait for my back, and give greater pain, for the unsuspected blow is the more fatal.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If again I have been a pilot, I have been one of the most skilful; the sea has been boisterous around us, boiling about the ship, and there has been considerable uproar among the passengers, who have always been fighting about something or another, and roaring against one another and the waves.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What a struggle I have had, seated at the helm, contending alike with the sea and the passengers, to bring the vessel safe to land through this double storm?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Had they in every way supported me, safety would have been hardly won, and when they were opposed to me, how has it been possible to avoid making shipwreck?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What more need be said?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But how can I bear this holy war?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For there has been said to be a holy, as well as a Persian, war.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: How shall I unite and join together the hostile occupants of sees, and hostile pastors, and the people broken up along with, and opposed to them, as if by some chasms caused by earthquakes between neighbouring and adjoining places; or as, in pestilential diseases, befalls servants and members of the family, when the sickness readily attacks in succession one after another; and besides the very quarters of the globe are affected by the spirit of faction, so that East and West are arrayed on opposite sides, and bid fair to be severed in opinion no less than in position.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: How long are parties to be mine and yours, the old and the new, the more rational and the more spiritual, the more noble and the more ignoble, the more and the less numerous?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I am ashamed of my old age, when, after being saved by Christ, I am called by the name of others.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I cannot bear your horse races and theatres, and this rage for rivalry in expense and party spirit.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: We unharness, and harness ourselves on the other side, we neigh against each other, we almost beat the air, as they do, and fling the dust towards heaven, like those which are excited; and under other masks satisfy our own rivalry, and become evil arbiters of emulation, and senseless judges of affairs.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: To-day sharing the same thrones and opinions, if our leaders thus carry us along; to-morrow hostile alike in position and opinion, if the wind blows in the contrary direction.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Amid the variations of friendship and hatred, our names also vary: and what is most terrible, we are not ashamed to set forth contrary doctrines to the same audience; nor are we constant to the same objects, being rendered different at different times by our contentiousness.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: They are like the ebb and flow of some narrow strait.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For as when the children are at play in the midst of the market place, it would be most disgraceful and unbecoming for us to leave our household business, and join them; for children’s toys are not becoming for old age: so, when others are contending, even if I am better informed than the majority, I could not allow myself to be one of them, rather than, as I now do, enjoy the freedom of obscurity.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For, besides all this, my feeling is that I do not, on most points, agree with the majority, and cannot bear to walk in the same way.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Rash and stupid though it may be, such is my feeling.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: That which is pleasant to others causes pain to me, and I am pleased with what is painful to others.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: So that I should not be surprised if I were even imprisoned as a disagreeable man, and thought by most men to be out of my senses, as is said to have been the case with one of the Greek philosophers, whose moderation exposed him to the charge of madness, because he laughed at everything, since he saw that the objects of the eager pursuit of the majority were ridiculous; or even be thought full of new wine as were in later days the disciples of Christ, because they spoke with tongues, since men knew not that it was the power of the Spirit, and not a distraction of mind.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Now, consider the charges laid against us.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: You have been ruler of the church, it is said, for so long, and favoured by the course of time, and the influence of the sovereign, a most important matter.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What change have we been able to notice?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: How many men have in days gone by used us outrageously?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What sufferings have we failed to undergo?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Confiscation?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The burning of priests at sea?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The desecration of temples by the blood of the saints, till, instead of temples, they became charnel-houses?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The public slaughter of aged Bishops, to speak more accurately, of Patriarchs?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The denial of access to every place in the case of the godly alone?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: In fact any kind of suffering which could be mentioned?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And for which of these have we requited the wrongdoers?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For the wheel of fortune gave us the power of rightly treating those who so treated us, and our persecutors ought to have received a lesson.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Apart from all other things, speaking only of our experiences, not to mention your own, have we not been persecuted, maltreated, driven from churches, houses, and, most terrible of all, even from the deserts?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Have we not had to endure an enraged people, insolent governors, the disregard of Emperors and their decrees?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What was the result?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: We became stronger, and our persecutors took to flight.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: That was actually the case.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The power to requite them seemed to me a sufficient vengeance on those who had wronged us.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: These men thought otherwise; for they are exceedingly exact and just in requiting: and accordingly they demand what the state of things permits.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What governor, they say, has been fined?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What populace chastised?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What ringleaders of the populace?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What fear of ourselves have we been able to inspire for the future?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Perhaps we may be reproached, as we have been before, with the exquisite character of our table, the splendour of our apparel, the officers who precede us, our haughtiness to those who meet us.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I was not aware that we ought to rival the consuls, the governors, the most illustrious generals, who have no opportunity of lavishing their incomes; or that our belly ought to hunger for the enjoyment of the goods of the poor, and to expend their necessaries on superfluities, and belch forth over the altars.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I did not know that we ought to ride on splendid horses, and drive in magnificent carriages, and be preceded by a procession and surrounded by applause, and have everyone make way for us, as if we were wild beasts, and open out a passage so that our approach might be seen afar.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If these sufferings have been endured, they have now passed away: Forgive me this wrong.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Elect another who will please the majority: and give me my desert, my country life, and my God, Whom alone I may have to please, and shall please by my simple life.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: It is a painful thing to be deprived of speeches and conferences, and public gatherings, and applause like that which now lends wings to my thoughts, and relatives, and friends and honours, and the beauty and grandeur of the city, and its brilliancy which dazzles those who look at the surface without investigating the inner nature of things; but yet not so painful as being clamoured against and besmirched amid public disturbances and agitations, which trim their sails to the popular breeze.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For they seek not for priests, but for orators, not for stewards of souls, but for treasurers of money, not for pure offerers of the sacrifice, but for powerful patrons.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I will say a word in their defence: we have thus trained them, by becoming all things to all men, whether to save or destroy all, I know not.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What say you?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Are you persuaded, have you been overcome by my words?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Or must I use stronger terms in order to persuade you?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Yea by the Trinity Itself, Whom you and I alike worship, by our common hope, and for the sake of the unity of this people, grant me this favour; dismiss me with your prayers; let this be the proclamation of my contest; give me my certificate of retirement, as sovereigns do to their soldiers; and, if you will, with a favourable testimony, that I may enjoy the honour of it; if not, just as you please; this will make no difference to me, until God sees what my case really is.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What successor then shall we elect?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: God will provide Himself a shepherd for the office, as He once provided a lamb for a burnt-offering.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I only make this further request,—let him be one who is the object of envy, not the object of pity; not one who yields everything to all, but one who can on some points offer resistance for the sake of what is best: for though the one is most pleasant, the other is most profitable.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: So do you prepare for me your addresses of dismissal: I will now bid you farewell.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Farewell my Anastasia, whose name is redolent of piety: for thou hast raised up for us the doctrine which was in contempt: farewell, scene of our common victory, modern Shiloh, where the tabernacle was first fixed, after being carried about in its wanderings for forty years in the wilderness.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Farewell likewise, grand and renowned temple, our new inheritance, whose greatness is now due to the Word, which once wast a Jebus, and hast now been made by us a Jerusalem.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Farewell, all ye others, inferior only to this in beauty, scattered through the various parts of the city, like so many links, uniting together each your own neighbourhood, which have been filled with worshippers of whose existence we had despaired, not by me, in my weakness, but by the grace which was with me.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Farewell, ye Apostles, noble settlers here, my masters in the strife; if I have not often kept festival with you, it has been possibly due to the Satan which I, like S.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Paul, who was one of you, carry about in my body for my own profit, and which is the cause of my now leaving you.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Farewell, my throne, envied and perilous height; farewell assembly of high priests, honoured by the dignity and age of its priests, and all ye others ministers of God round the holy table, drawing nigh to the God Who draws nigh to you.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Farewell, choirs of Nazarites, harmonies of the Psalter, night-long stations, venerable virgins, decorous matrons, gatherings of widows and orphans, and ye eyes of the poor, turned towards God and towards me.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Farewell, hospitable and Christ-loved dwellings, helpers of my infirmity.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Farewell, ye lovers of my discourses, in your eagerness and concourse, ye pencils seen and unseen, and thou balustrade, pressed upon by those who thrust themselves forward to hear the word.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Farewell, Emperors, and palace, and ministers and household of the Emperor, whether faithful or not to him, I know not, but for the most part, unfaithful to God.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Clap your hands, shout aloud, extol your orator to the skies.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: This pestilent and garrulous tongue has ceased to speak to you.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Though it will not utterly cease to speak: for it will fight with hand and ink: but for the present we have ceased to speak.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Farewell, mighty Christ-loving city.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I will testify to the truth, though thy zeal be not according to knowledge.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Our separation renders us more kindly.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Approach the truth: be converted at this late hour.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Honour God more than you have been wont to do.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: It is no disgrace to change, while it is fatal to cling to evil.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Farewell, East and West, for whom and against whom I have had to fight; He is witness, Who will give you peace, if but a few would imitate my retirement.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For those who resign their thrones will not also lose God, but will have the seat on high, which is far more exalted and secure.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Last of all, and most of all, I will cry,—farewell ye Angels, guardians of this church, and of my presence and pilgrimage, since our affairs are in the hands of God.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Farewell, O Trinity, my meditation, and my glory.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Mayest Thou be preserved by those who are here, and preserve them, my people: for they are mine, even if I have my place assigned elsewhere; and may I learn that Thou art ever extolled and glorified in word and conduct.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: My children, keep, I pray you, that which is committed to your trust.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Remember my stonings.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all.
